In attempting to characterize the epimorphisms in the
category of Hausdorff topological groups, one is led to investi­
II gating certain quotients of the free product G G of a Haus­
dorff topological group with itself. In particular, one
II wants to know if the amalgamated free product G G, for a
closed subgroup B, is Hausdorff, or if its Hausdorff reflection
is "sufficiently large." To do this it seems useful to obtain
II information about the topological structure of G G, or more
